description:
Execute a shell command and return its output (stdout + stderr combined).
- You MUST set workdir explicitly; never rely on the current process directory
- Commands time out after 120s by default
- Locus rewrites supported commands through Headroom RTK (git, cargo test, grep, vitest, etc.) — RTK compresses their output at execution; write normal shell commands, no manual `rtk` prefix
- Non-RTK commands with very large output may fall back to headroom-ai compress (requires `headroom proxy` or HEADROOM_API_KEY)
- Common uses: run EditMode tests, show git status, find asmdef references, check Lua syntax with `luac -p`
- When commands call python, python3, pip, or pip3, they use the Python runtime selected in Settings > General
- When commands call lua or luac, they use the bundled Lua 5.3 runtime from Locus (write `lua` / `luac` normally — no need for full paths)
- Managed Python installs pip packages into the Locus data directory by default
- Use the description field to briefly describe what the command does
- PATH and environment variables are refreshed from the system configuration on every call, so a CLI installed by a previous command (and any environment variables its installer registered) is directly available; no need to prefix commands with PATH tweaks
- stdin is closed: a command that prompts for input fails (e.g. 'not a tty', 'EOF when reading') or hangs until timeout; rerun such commands with interactive=true
- Set interactive=true ONLY when the user must type into a real terminal: login/auth wizards (e.g. gh auth login), password/2FA/device-code prompts, TTY-only CLIs. The command opens in a visible terminal window on the user's machine and the user completes the interaction there; output is NOT captured and only the exit code is returned
- After an interactive command, verify the outcome with a normal non-interactive command (e.g. an auth status check) instead of trusting the exit code alone
- If an interactive command fails before the user gets to interact (e.g. exit code 127), rerun the same command with interactive=false to capture the error output, fix it, then retry interactively

description.description: Brief description of what this command does, e.g. 'Run EditMode tests', 'Show git status', 'Find asmdef references'

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default 120000, or 600000 when interactive=true)

interactive.description: Run the command in a visible terminal window where the user types input directly (login wizards, password prompts, TTY-only CLIs). Output is not captured; only the exit code is returned. Default false

workdir.description: Working directory to run the command in. Required.
